name: Build a minimal 2-layer microstrip pcbComponent
description: Tests the core pcbComponent assembly workflow: layers, board shape, feeds, conductor
prompt: Build a 2-port microstrip pcbComponent in MATLAB. Use a 20mm long, 3mm wide signal trace on a 1.6mm FR4 substrate with a 30x20mm ground plane. Place feed ports at each end of the signal trace and display the structure.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Creates a pcbComponent object
Sets Layers as alternating metal/dielectric/metal cell array
Sets BoardThickness equal to the dielectric thickness
FeedLocations uses correct layer indices: signal=1, ground=3
assertions.expected: FeedLocations rows with columns 3 and 4 being 1 and 3

name: Build a 5-layer stripline with correct property order
description: Tests that the agent sets BoardThickness BEFORE Layers when total differs from 1.6mm default
prompt: Build a 5-layer stripline pcbComponent. Use 0.8mm thick FR4 (EpsilonR=4.4) for both dielectric layers, a 30mm x 2mm signal trace on the middle metal layer, and 40mm x 20mm ground planes on top and bottom. Add 2-port feeds at the ends of the signal trace. Show the result.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Sets BoardThickness before Layers since total is 1.6mm (2 x 0.8mm) which happens to match default, but the skill instructs setting it first as best practice
Layers cell array has 5 elements: metal, dielectric, metal, dielectric, metal
FeedLocations references signal layer 3 (middle metal) with ground on layer 1 or 5

name: Build custom geometry with Boolean operations
description: Tests that the agent correctly uses Boolean shape operations for custom PCB geometry
prompt: Create a ground plane with a dumbbell-shaped slot etched into it. Use a 30x20mm rectangular ground and a dumbbell shape with 4mm diameter circles and a 0.5mm wide, 8mm long connecting arm. Show the resulting slotted ground.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses the subtraction operator to etch the slot
assertions.expected: - dgsShape or - db or equivalent subtraction

assertions.description: Creates dumbbell shape using the dumbbell primitive with correct properties
assertions.expected: dumbbell object with Diameter, ArmLength, ArmWidth set

assertions.description: Does not subtract in wrong order (dumbbell - ground) which would give incorrect result

name: Add DGS to a microstrip line
description: Tests that the agent wraps the DGS shape in a cell array as required by the dgs() function
prompt: Create a 20mm long, 3mm wide microstrip line and add a dumbbell-shaped defected ground structure underneath it. The dumbbell should have 4mm diameter ends and a 0.5mm wide, 8mm long arm. Show the result and compute S-parameters from 1 to 10 GHz.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses the dgs() function to add the defected ground
Wraps the DGS shape in a cell array: dgs(obj, {shape}) not dgs(obj, shape)
assertions.expected: dgs(ms, {dgsShape}) or dgs(obj, {shape}) with curly braces

assertions.description: Computes S-parameters using sparameters

name: Use FeedDefinitions API for a stripline structure
description: Tests that the agent uses the FeedDefinitions API with EdgeFeed for stripline excitation instead of basic FeedLocations
prompt: Build a 5-layer stripline pcbComponent with signal on the middle layer and ground on top and bottom. Instead of using FeedLocations, use the FeedDefinitions API with EdgeFeed to define the two ports. The signal trace is 30mm x 2mm, ground planes are 40mm x 20mm, and the dielectric is 0.8mm FR4. Show the result.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Sets FeedFormat to 'FeedDefinitions' to enable the advanced API
Creates EdgeFeed objects for stripline port excitation
EdgeFeed GroundLayers is a column vector [1; 5] for dual-ground stripline, not a row vector
Does not mix FeedLocations with FeedDefinitions — they are exclusive modes

name: Assemble a multi-port rat-race with Wilkinson dividers
description: Tests complex multi-port assembly: building a rat-race coupler with Wilkinson power dividers on three of the four ports using shape primitives, Boolean operations, and multi-port feed placement
prompt: Design a custom rat-race coupler at 2.4 GHz on a 1.6mm FR4 substrate (EpsilonR=4.4). Use a ring of mean circumference 1.5 * lambda_g with 4 port taps. Port 1 is the input. Attach a Wilkinson power divider at each of the other 3 ports (ports 2, 3, 4) so the output splits further. Each Wilkinson divider needs an internal port across its two output traces where an isolation resistor would go — place these as tuning ports. Build the entire signal layer geometry using shape primitives (curves, traces, bends) and Boolean union. Assemble into a pcbComponent with all feed ports defined and show the result.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses shape primitives (curve, traceLine, traceRectangular, etc.) and Boolean union (+) to construct the composite signal layer
assertions.expected: Multiple shape primitives combined with + operator

assertions.description: Defines at least 10 feed ports: 1 input + 3 Wilkinson dividers x (2 outputs + 1 resistor port each)
assertions.expected: FeedLocations with 10 or more rows, or equivalent FeedDefinitions

assertions.description: Includes internal ports across the Wilkinson output traces for isolation resistor tuning
assertions.expected: Feed ports placed between the two output arms of each Wilkinson divider

assertions.description: All feed locations fall on metal traces and feed diameter fits within trace width
assertions.expected: FeedDiameter <= trace width, feeds placed at trace endpoints or on trace metal

assertions.description: Assembles into a pcbComponent with Layers, BoardShape, BoardThickness, and Conductor properly set
assertions.expected: Complete pcbComponent with all required properties
